jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh! scream during title sequence mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer signature moment - perfectly integrated character comedy but routine by this point in series

jokes.text: No es bueno
jokes.setup: Bart playing violent arcade games
jokes.punchline: Casual Spanish phrase commentary on the violence
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Bart's casual attitude toward arcade violence

jokes.text: Winners don't use drugs. Ehh! Ehh! Ehh! Ehh! Ehh! Ehh! Ehh! Ehh!
jokes.setup: Anti-drug message playing on arcade machine
jokes.punchline: Machine immediately breaks down/malfunctions after the PSA
jokes.explanation: Good satirical observation about the irony of broken-down arcade machines delivering anti-drug messages

jokes.text: Don't say that out loud. Two Caucasian males out of money in Sector 4. Go to code red.
jokes.setup: Kids running out of arcade money
jokes.punchline: Treating broke kids like a security emergency with military precision
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - treating mundane kid problem like military crisis

jokes.text: I'm sorry. You'll have to leave.
jokes.setup: Kids being kicked out of arcade
jokes.punchline: Employee's voice breaking/cracking while delivering authority
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy of authority figure undermined by adolescent voice

jokes.text: You mustn't kill time, boys. You must cherish it. Seize the day! Can I have some change to go get loaded?
jokes.setup: Inspiring philosophical advice about cherishing time
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for money to buy alcohol
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection joke with perfect structure - philosophical wisdom immediately undercut by crass request

jokes.text: Honey Roasted Peanuts. Ingredients: Salt... artificial honey-roasting agents... pressed peanut sweepings.
jokes.setup: Homer reading snack ingredients as literature
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting ingredients treated as fine reading
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's simple pleasures and the gross reality of processed food

jokes.text: Steak? Money's too tight for steak. Steak? Mm, sure. Steak.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining they can't afford steak
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately agreeing to steak anyway, showing he wasn't listening
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer not-listening bit, perfectly executed character comedy

jokes.text: The last peanut-- overflowing with the oil and salt of its departed brothers.
jokes.setup: Homer eating the final peanut
jokes.punchline: Treating snack consumption like epic poetry/war memorial
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - Homer's grandiose language for mundane snack eating

jokes.text: Hmm. Ow! Pointy. Eww! Slimy. Uh-oh! Moving. Aha! Aw. Twenty dollars.
jokes.setup: Homer investigating something in the peanut jar
jokes.punchline: Discovery of twenty dollars after experiencing various unpleasant textures
jokes.explanation: Good escalating physical comedy with economical dialogue showing Homer's discovery process

jokes.text: I wanted a peanut. Twenty dollars can buy many peanuts. Explain how. Money can be exchanged for goods and services.
jokes.setup: Homer disappointed about not getting a peanut
jokes.punchline: Brain having to explain basic economics to Homer
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons joke - Homer's complete lack of understanding of basic concepts, perfectly structured internal dialogue

jokes.text: This is wearin' a bit thin. You think so?
jokes.setup: Extended slapstick sequence
jokes.punchline: Characters commenting on the comedy wearing thin
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke acknowledging when physical comedy goes on too long

jokes.text: Our prayers have been answered.
jokes.setup: Kids seeing money falling from sky
jokes.punchline: Treating found money as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic reaction - kids treating luck as religious miracle

jokes.text: One that's made entirely out of syrup. Entirely ou-- [Gasps]
jokes.setup: Bart ordering a Super-Squishy
jokes.punchline: Apu's horror at the impossible/dangerous request
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist setup showing kids' naive excess and Apu's professional concern

jokes.text: An all-syrup Super-Squishy? Oh, sir, such a thing has never been done. Just make it happen.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining this is unprecedented
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal of impossibility/danger
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Apu's professional concern vs Bart's childish determination

jokes.text: Oh! She won't hold! She's breaking up!
jokes.setup: Slushy machine being pushed beyond limits
jokes.punchline: Apu treating machine like sinking ship with dramatic nautical language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke treating convenience store equipment failure as maritime disaster

jokes.text: Okay. We're young, rich and full of sugar. What'll we do? Let's go crazy Broadway-style!
jokes.setup: Kids high on sugar with money to spend
jokes.punchline: Deciding to act out Broadway musical fantasy
jokes.explanation: Setup for musical number - kids' sugar-fueled grandiose plans

jokes.text: Springfield, Springfield It's a helluva town / The school yard's up and the shopping mall's down
jokes.setup: Parodying 'New York, New York' from 'On the Town'
jokes.punchline: Mundane Springfield geography treated as exciting NYC features
jokes.explanation: Decent parody song replacing NYC glamour with Springfield mundanity

jokes.text: New York is thataway, man. Thanks, kid.
jokes.setup: Kids singing about Springfield being like New York
jokes.punchline: Actual New Yorker asking for directions, showing they're nowhere near NYC
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke puncturing the kids' NYC fantasy with reality

jokes.text: Mmm! Free goo.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering something edible
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for unidentified sticky substance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food enthusiasm - will eat anything if it's free

jokes.text: Hey! Don't bogart that Squishy! I don't know where you magic pixies came from... but I like your pixie drink.
jokes.setup: Homer encountering sugar-high kids
jokes.punchline: Homer seeing kids as magical creatures due to his own altered state
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Homer's altered perception meeting sugar-high kids

jokes.text: The remorse of the sugar junkie.
jokes.setup: Bart waking up with sugar crash
jokes.punchline: Lisa's clinical diagnosis of sugar hangover
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa line - her intellectual framing of childhood excess

jokes.text: Oh, no! I must have joined the Junior Campers. The few, the proud, the geeky.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering he joined scouts while sugar-high
jokes.punchline: Parody of Marine Corps slogan 'The Few, The Proud' with 'geeky'
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of military recruiting slogan applied to boy scouts

jokes.text: Boy! A man on a Squishy bender can sure do some crazy things.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on sugar-induced poor decisions
jokes.punchline: Treating sugar high like alcoholic bender
jokes.explanation: Good parallel between sugar rush and alcoholic behavior

jokes.text: I've made my bed, and now I've gotta weasel out of it.
jokes.setup: Bart acknowledging his predicament
jokes.punchline: Corrupting 'made my bed, now lie in it' idiom with 'weasel out'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay corrupting common idiom to match Bart's character - avoids responsibility

jokes.text: Weaseling out of things is important to learn. It's what separates us from the animals-- except the weasel.
jokes.setup: Homer giving parental advice about responsibility
jokes.punchline: Promoting irresponsibility as human evolution, then literal weasel exception
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wisdom - completely wrong moral lesson delivered with confidence, plus perfect animal logic

jokes.text: I got a dirty word shaved into the back of my head. What is it with you kids and that word?
jokes.setup: Milhouse admitting to having profanity shaved in hair
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperation suggesting it's a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's frustrated familiarity with kids' behavior

jokes.text: Oh, no. Woe is me. My precious uniform. Oh.
jokes.setup: Bart forced to pretend he wants his uniform back
jokes.punchline: Completely unconvincing fake distress
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Bart's terrible acting and lack of commitment

jokes.text: Um, Mrs. Krabappel, I'd like to stay... but this uniform carries certain responsibilities.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get out of test to attend scout meeting
jokes.punchline: Suddenly taking scout responsibilities seriously when convenient
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Bart suddenly embracing scout duty to avoid schoolwork

jokes.text: Hello, alternative to testing.
jokes.setup: Bart entering scout meeting
jokes.punchline: Cynically acknowledging scouts as just test avoidance
jokes.explanation: Good economical line showing Bart's purely mercenary attitude toward scouting

jokes.text: You're just in time for Sponge-bath the Old Folks Day. Help yourself, but stay above the equator.
jokes.setup: Scout activity involving elderly care
jokes.punchline: Anatomical boundaries for inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable scout activity with awkward boundaries - typical cringe setup

jokes.text: Well, sir, just apply a smidgen of peanut butter to an ordinary pine cone... and you've got yourself a makeshift bird feeder, sir.
jokes.setup: Scout demonstrating craft project
jokes.punchline: Overly formal presentation of simple craft project
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of scout earnestness - overly serious tone for simple activity

jokes.text: Oh, cool. A 'spork.' Don't hurt me!
jokes.setup: Scouts getting pocketknives, Bart gets spork
jokes.punchline: Other scout's fear of plastic utensil
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke - wimpy scouts afraid of harmless plastic while Bart appreciates it

jokes.text: You call that a knife? This is a knife.
jokes.setup: Scout knife discussion
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Crocodile Dundee' movie with actual dangerous knife
jokes.explanation: Direct movie quote reference - relies on external recognition rather than original humor

jokes.text: This man's appendix is about to burst. Luckily, I have my trusty pocketknife.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency scenario
jokes.punchline: Professional doctor using scout knife for surgery
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - medical professional relying on scout equipment

jokes.text: 'Don't do what Donny Don't does.' They could've made this clearer.
jokes.setup: Bart reading confusing safety manual
jokes.punchline: Double negative creating genuinely confusing instruction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke about confusing safety instructions - genuinely unclear language

jokes.text: Well! If it isn't the leader of the wiener patrol... boning up on his nerd lessons.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Bart studying scout material
jokes.punchline: Childish insults toward educational effort
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer anti-intellectual humor - predictable character moment

jokes.text: 'Egghead likes his booky-wook'
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to mock Bart's studying
jokes.punchline: Baby-talk mockery of reading
jokes.explanation: More basic Homer anti-intellectualism - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Rubber knife? This place is for wimps.
jokes.setup: Bart receiving rubber training knife
jokes.punchline: Bart's disgust at safety measures
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart reaction to authority/safety - predictable character response

jokes.text: Ooh! Floor pie.
jokes.setup: Homer spotting food on ground
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for questionable found food
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food enthusiasm - perfectly economical and character-specific

jokes.text: The guys who wrote this show don't know squat. Itchy should've tied Scratchy's tongue with a taut-line hitch, not a sheet bend.
jokes.setup: Bart watching violent cartoon
jokes.punchline: Technical knot-tying criticism of cartoon violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke showing how scout knowledge changes Bart's perspective on everything, including cartoon violence

jokes.text: Howdilly-hey, Camper Bart! Ready for today's meeting? You know-dilly know it, Neddy. Okilly-dokilly.
jokes.setup: Ned's characteristic speech pattern
jokes.punchline: Bart mockingly adopting Ned's -illy suffix pattern
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Bart sarcastically mirroring Ned's speech pattern

jokes.text: Duh, I'm the captain. My son is Bart. What an oaf. How embarrassing. I'm glad he's not my father.
jokes.setup: Homer introducing himself and Bart
jokes.punchline: Other parents' immediate negative reaction to Homer
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's social awkwardness and others' instant judgment

jokes.text: Hiya! I'm sure you kids know me best... as Sergeant Fatso Judson in From Here to Eternity.
jokes.setup: Celebrity guest introducing himself to kids
jokes.punchline: Assuming kids know him from 1950s war movie rather than modern work
jokes.explanation: Decent age-gap humor about celebrity assuming kids know his old work

jokes.text: How was jerk practice, boy? Did they teach you how to sing to trees... and build crappy furniture out of useless wooden logs?
jokes.setup: Homer asking about scout activities
jokes.punchline: Mocking outdoor skills as pointless while describing them accurately
jokes.explanation: Good Homer anti-outdoors sentiment showing his urban bias against nature skills

jokes.text: Look. Homer won't wanna go. So just ask him, and he'll say no. I don't wanna go. So if he asks me to go, I'll just say yes.
jokes.setup: Both planning to use reverse psychology
jokes.punchline: Their plans perfectly defeating each other
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for mutual reverse psychology backfire - both too clever for their own good

jokes.text: Wait! Are you sure that's how this sort of thing works? Shut up, brain, or I'll stab you with a Q-tip.
jokes.setup: Homer's brain questioning his logic
jokes.punchline: Homer threatening his own brain with cotton swab violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vs. brain moment - absurd threat to his own intelligence with mundane household item

jokes.text: Bart, I'd be delighted to go on your trip with you. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Their reverse psychology plans activating
jokes.punchline: Both getting exactly what they didn't want
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the reverse psychology setup - both plans backfiring simultaneously

jokes.text: Arr! Here be a fine vessel... the yarest river-goin' boat there be. I'll take it. Arr. I don't know what I'm doin'.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain selling boat with nautical expertise
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Sea Captain's confidence immediately undercut by honest admission

jokes.text: Please not Flanders. Please not Flanders. Please not Flanders. Well, Homer, looks like we're boat buddies, huh?
jokes.setup: Homer desperately hoping to avoid Ned
jokes.punchline: Getting exactly what he feared most
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-payoff for character pairing Homer dreads - predictable but effective

jokes.text: I guess now we know why... they call 'em rapids and not 'slowpids,' huh?
jokes.setup: Encountering dangerous river rapids
jokes.punchline: Ned's terrible pun in crisis situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - terrible dad joke at inappropriate time

jokes.text: Hey! There's a New Mexico. So many Krusty Burger locations.
jokes.setup: Looking at map for navigation help
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on fast food locations instead of geography
jokes.explanation: Good Homer prioritization joke - food more important than survival navigation

jokes.text: So many Krusty Burger locations.
jokes.setup: Homer studying map during emergency
jokes.punchline: Only noticing fast food restaurants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priority moment - food locations more interesting than navigation during crisis

jokes.text: Wrong again, Flanders. In a situation like this, you just relax... and let the current take you back to land.
jokes.setup: Homer confidently contradicting Ned's survival advice
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong advice delivered with total confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overconfidence leading them further into danger

jokes.text: Oh, pardon me, Mr. Let's Ration Everything. What do you think we're floatin' on? Don't you know the poem? Water, water everywhere, so let's all have a drink.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to conserve fresh water
jokes.punchline: Homer corrupting 'Ancient Mariner' poem about undrinkable seawater
jokes.explanation: Excellent literary reference corruption - Homer misremembering famous poem about dangerous water

jokes.text: We're saved! Seagulls always stay near land. They only go out to sea to die.
jokes.setup: Ned excited about seeing seagulls
jokes.punchline: His animal knowledge actually makes situation worse
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - hope immediately crushed by additional information

jokes.text: See that, boy? Your old man was right, not Flanders. We are doomed. In your face, Flanders!
jokes.setup: Homer vindicated about being doomed
jokes.punchline: Celebrating being right about their doom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - competitive about being right even when it means they'll die

jokes.text: See that, boy? Your old man was right, not Flanders. We are doomed. In your face, Flanders!
jokes.setup: Homer being vindicated about their dire situation
jokes.punchline: Celebrating being right about them all dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - being right matters more than survival

jokes.text: Look here, boy. The secret is to take tiny bites, like so. Mmm! Mmm! Mmm!
jokes.setup: Homer teaching rationing by example
jokes.punchline: Immediately eating all the food while demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-control failure - good intentions immediately sabotaged by appetite

jokes.text: I bet they're having the time of their life. [Deliverance banjo music]
jokes.setup: Bart imagining other rafts are doing well
jokes.punchline: Sinister 'Deliverance' music suggesting they're in worse danger
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference to famous movie about river trip gone wrong

jokes.text: This ain't one of your church picnic flare gun firings, Flanders. This is the real thing.
jokes.setup: Homer taking charge of emergency flare
jokes.punchline: Treating Ned like amateur at flare usage
jokes.explanation: Good Homer overconfidence moment - assuming expertise he doesn't have

jokes.text: We're done-didilly-dodilly done-didilly-dodilly done-didilly-dodilly done--
jokes.setup: Ned having breakdown about their situation
jokes.punchline: Breakdown expressed through his characteristic speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific breakdown - Ned's crisis expressed through his linguistic quirks

jokes.text: We're done-didilly-dodilly done-didilly-dodilly done-didilly-dodilly done-- - Flanders! Snap out of it!
jokes.setup: Ned having complete breakdown with his speech pattern
jokes.punchline: Homer having to be the rational one stopping Ned's spiral
jokes.explanation: Ned's verbal tic becoming symptom of breakdown, role reversal with Homer

jokes.text: Godspeed, little doodle.
jokes.setup: Homer using last food as fishing bait
jokes.punchline: Solemn farewell to cheese doodle
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - epic farewell to junk food, perfect Homer sentimentality

jokes.text: I stole it from that Borgnine guy.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Bart meaningful Swiss Army knife gift
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft from celebrity guest
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's touching gesture immediately undercut by criminal confession

jokes.text: Thanks, boy. Now, be careful when you-- Oops.
jokes.setup: Homer giving safety advice about the knife
jokes.punchline: Immediately dropping it dangerously
jokes.explanation: Standard safety irony - advice immediately contradicted by action

jokes.text: The foul stench of death is upon us! Mmm! Hamburger.
jokes.setup: Bart dramatically announcing their doom
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizing 'death stench' as delicious food
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - death becomes salvation through Homer's food obsession

jokes.text: Never mind the fog! That way! Steer. There isn't much time!
jokes.setup: Homer following scent of food to salvation
jokes.punchline: Treating food discovery with military urgency
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food urgency - survival instinct channeled through appetite

jokes.text: Give me 700 Krusty Burgers! You want fries with that?
jokes.setup: Starving Homer reaching fast food restaurant
jokes.punchline: Employee's casual upselling despite massive order
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist customer service moment - routine upselling despite absurd circumstances
